preamble: The shift from a vowel [a] to a nasal consonant [n] often occurs via an intermediate stage of nasalization. In some contexts, a nasalized vowel 'unpacks' or resolves back into a full nasal consonant, a process known as re-consonantalization.

examples.note: The French nasalized vowel resolved to a vowel + nasal consonant in English.
Nasal excrescence: insertion of [n] after the vowel.

sources: Pope: From Latin to Modern French
